a11yRisks: Small metadata lines on parchment surfaces should still be watched in hover/selected states if more palette weathering is introduced.
No deterministic contrast audit or visual diff is part of this loop, so the final decision is still judgment-based.
issues: The bestiary page is closer after fusing the specimen into the folio, but the page still reads a bit more composed and polished than the reference. The source image relies on denser incidental ornament and a slightly more incidental task rhythm.

The refactor page improved in the middle-to-lower transition, but the lower ledger and diff register are still a touch more rectangular and evenly spaced than the original manuscript-like composition.

Palette hierarchy is strong now, yet a few brass and teal accents still read slightly cleaner and less weathered than the reference images, especially around the specimen framing and footer ornament run.
nextEdits: Push one more fidelity pass on the refactor lower register so the diff and summary band feel slightly less boxed and more manually composed.
Increase decorative incidental density on the bestiary page around the specimen and lower tool rows without turning it into noise.
Weather a few brass/teal accents and tighten footer ornament spacing so the surface lands closer to the original images' aged manuscript tone.
strengths: The Seraph kit is materially reusable now, with better shared vocabulary for manuscript hero surfaces and irregular folio rhythm.
The bestiary dominant artifact no longer feels boxed off as a modern card and is much closer to the reference family.
Storybook/example parity remains intact because the changes stayed inside the shared Seraph rendering path.
A11y-sensitive contrast improvements from previous passes still hold while the manuscript palette remains legible and distinctive.
